Spokane's population is increasing
with steady strides. Several thou
sand additional people are found in
the city each year.


Present prospects indicate an enor-
nous crop In the Inland Empire this
year. Probably both the yield and
the acreage will be the greatest in
the history of the state. Prospects
for good prices also are bright. This
makes Spokane's prosperity assured.
Besides this, the other resources of
| the country tributary to Spokane are
in at least fair condition and some
of them are looking better than for
some time. The prospects for the
lumber business are better than they
have been and mining In some of
the camps Is on n better foundation
than for two or three years.
! This accounts for the fact that the
business depression felt In other
and for the fact that buildings are
being erected in every part of tho city
and find eager tenants.
The population has increased about
80 per cent according to the school
| census.
